g.data-qualified-name: Shared . Cross-Task Classes.n.not owned by a single task..QueueManagementSystem
Shared . Cross-Task Classes.n.not owned by a single task..GMN32
g.text: "appears only in relations; not owned by any task"
Relationship cardinalities corrected by LLM review
Reason: PlantUML "-->" is a directed association but lacks cardinality; the corrected relationship indicates a queue item may have zero or more change events, not the other way around.
Reason: A one-to-many aggregation from Patient to UserAuthenticationSystem is incorrect; a patient uses a single authentication system, so a simple one-to-one association is appropriate.
Reason: A dependency suggests a weaker relationship but the domain requires the database to own symptom records; corrected to an aggregation from database to records.
Reason: The arrow "*--" indicates many on left, but Queue should own many QueueItems; reversal corrects to composition where Queue has many items.
Reason: A queue contains queue items (not patients directly) and the ordered list constraint is better modeled as a composition with an ordering annotation; correcting the right class to QueueItem.

Reason: REQ_ITEM_URGENCY_01 likely represents a requirement or use case, not directly associated with Actor, Permission, or State; it should only relate to UrgencyLevel as a requirement specification.
Reason: Same as above; all other associations for REQ_ITEM_URGENCY_01 except to UrgencyLevel are inappropriate for this domain.
Reason: Same as above; only the association with UrgencyLevel is relevant.
Reason: Same as above; only UrgencyLevel association is intended.
Reason: Same as above; only UrgencyLevel association is intended.
Reason: A resource is associated with zero or more actors (users), not necessarily exactly one; the cardinality should reflect optionality.
Reason: Same as above; the multiplicity on the right should be 0..* rather than 'many' which is ambiguous.
Reason: A resource should have exactly one urgency level, not an optional 0..1; the task requires each item to have an urgency.

Reason: A symptom is a single occurrence and corresponds to exactly one symptom record; the original optionality suggests a symptom may not have a record, which contradicts the registration requirement.
Reason: The aggregation direction and cardinality are reversed; a symptom record is aggregated into at most one patient database, but a database can contain many records.
[missing_relationship] There is no relationship between Patient and SymptomRecord, yet Task #53 requires recording symptom information.
[missing_relationship] There is no relationship between Queue and Patient, yet Tasks #55, #56, #57 involve a queue of patients.
[missing_relationship] There is no relationship between ChangeEvent and Queue, but Task #56 requires queue reordering on change.

[introduce_value_object] Consider making
UrgencyLevel a value object (e.g., with range
1-5 and validation) instead of an enum, to
better encapsulate its behavior and constraints.

[rename_for_clarity] Rename
'REQ_ITEM_URGENCY_01' to a descriptive name like
'ItemUrgency' to align with ubiquitous language
and improve clarity.
